raw_input:
[5.0/5] High Quality Product That Delivers High Quality Results "Best shave I've ever had. Gives me a close shave with no tugging. Better than an electric razor".

That's the first thing my husband had to say after he tested SUPRENT's Matrix Cutter Head travel kit razor for the first time. And what a kit this is. A razor handle, 2-blade refills (each with the first of its kind 6 ultra-thin blades total!), a dock to hold the razor and a bottle of shaving foam are included.

The handle is sleek and heavy but easy and comfortable to hold. The head doesn't pivot but it doesn't need to thanks to the 38.5 degree shaving angle. It glides perfectly along the jaw line and chin areas without missing a spot.

The base is heavy-ish and holds the razor well. Doesn't tip or wobble.

He's not a fan of the shaving foam. Says he must 'really rub it in' in order for it to foam. He used it once and switched back to his regular brand.

Suprent has really come out with an awesome razor kit. The presentation is gorgeous and the product works exceptionally well. Without a doubt this is worthy of gift giving. The price is reasonable.

Highly recommended [3.0/5] Some Good and Some Bad This is one of those products what tries to wow you with great packaging and high quality materials. Seriously, the packaging for this thing is better than a $450 watch I got recently. It's top notch.

RAZOR HANDLE - C
This thing is so heavy it borders on ridiculous. It's solid metal. If you dropped it on your foot, it would hurt. It feels good in your hand until you try to use it. There are no good grip points and the entire thing is smooth. Once it gets wet, it's a nightmare to hold and use.

BLADES - A
The blades are some of the best you'll find. Super close shave and they last a number of shaves. You really can't beat them.

CREAM - F
Garbage. This stuff is better than nothing, but that's about it. I used it once and then threw it out.

BASE - A
Yes you get a base to hold your razor. It's heavy, has a rubber bottom and it stays put.

USEABILITY - D-
Bad. If they have the best blades, how is it bad? The head that holds the blade is so big, that it gets in the way. Why this is not an issue for most parts of your face, it's a big issue when trying to shave under your nose. I had to grab a second razor to reach that area. They went for form over function with this one.

If you want a super heavy razor, that will last forever, then skip this one. You are better off with a blade from Supply. They do a very similar thing. Big high end handle and then try to survive off of the blades. The big difference is, their razor can shave your whole face. [5.0/5] Nice looking kit This is nice looking kit with good shaving cream. Only complaint is I wish the razors you could buy in the store and pop them in versus ordering online and having them shipped. [1.0/5] Form Over Function, to the Extreme Most of the products that I receive through the Amazon Vine program are decent to excellent, but I sometimes get really bad ones. I never let the fact that I receive the products free affect my ratings. I always call the shots as I see them. This SUPRENT razor is the latest example; its stunning manifestation of idiotic product design catapulted it in one fell swoop to the top of my ignominious list of “Worst Products E-V-E-R!”

It’s so bad that I shan’t waste time enumerating its faults in detail, or in trying to be humorous or clever as I usually do in my reviews. This stinker doesn’t even warrant the effort it takes to make a bulletized list, but herewith I reluctantly give it that much attention:

- It’s too heavy to maneuver around the face or wherever.
- The head is too big for precise shaving in tight areas such as under the nose.
- The “38.5° Golden Shaving Angle” is the wrong angle for just about every surface.
- The worst of its many bone-headed “features” is that the chrome-plated zinc handle is too thin for a firm grip; it slips, slides and spins with Teflon-like frictionlessness in the hand, which is not only annoying but actually dangerous. How could it not occur to the geniuses who designed it that a mirror-smooth chromed metal surface with absolutely NO knurling, stippling or any other texture would be even MORE slippery when held in a wet hand with soapy shaving cream on it? Did anyone even try it out on a real face before they shipped it out the door?
- Speaking of shaving cream, the little can that comes in this set is useless. It’s as light and insubstantial as NASA Aerogel, and disappears to a thin oily layer when rubbed in. I don’t know what it is, but it’s NOT like any other shaving cream that I’ve ever used.
- The huge, unwieldy, six-blade head does NOT glide smoothly across the skin; the blades catch and grab at every opportunity, and do NOT shave closely.
- Since no blade replacements show up on Amazon, they’re clearly rare, hard to find and, if you can find them, no doubt they’re obscenely expensive.
- The stylish, oh-so-elegant “portfolio” that accompanies this set is the most pretentious thing that I’ve seen in a long time. I mean, it’s a RAZOR, not the latest iPad Pro!

Anyone who spends 50 bucks for this set rather than getting it for free will surely be wildly disappointed and probably furious once they realize what they got for their money. It’s not worth 50 cents. It’s all about form over function, with form checking in at 100% and function zero.

In the past, I’ve gotten some marginal razors through Vine, but none have been so bad that I’ve thrown them away rather than using them. This one is the exception.

The minimum-allowed one-star rating is far too generous for this “object” that, whatever it is, doesn’t qualify as a “razor” in any practical way. Caveat emptor! [5.0/5] An upgrade of user experience! [[VIDEOID:d8c41a00f42f082c27a08126befb6d7c]] I bought this for my boyfriend as a gift, initially drawn to the look only. Unpacking was enjoyable with a strong sense of luxe. The razor is so sculptural that we couldn’t help but take a bunch photos for IG... He has used many razors before and thinks what’s unique for this product is that the handle itself gives it some weight so it’s really effortless when making strokes. The round holder for the razor is a nice touch so you can place it on any surface that you want. It comes with one blade replacement. We decided to record a video for this review becuase he really likes the product. You can also see the before/after comparison in one of the photos. It would be interesting to see this model as a safety razor, too, If we are to give any suggestions. [4.0/5] Unique. . .Maybe Too Unique This is a really neat razor that has one possible issue, which I'll get to later.

The handle is big and heavy, and its unusual shape is very stylish. It gives a nice shave, too. If you have a small face or tight features, the large head might be a bit tricky to use in some areas, but that hasn't been a major issue. I usually use an old-school double-edge razor, not a flexing cartridge razor, so the fixed head angle isn't an issue for me. If you're used to a razor with a movable head, this one might take some getting used to.

The included shaving foam smells good and works well. You don't get a lot, but you don't have to use a large amount of it.

The "stand" is just a heavy metal cylinder with a round hole in the top and a pad on the bottom. The hole is wider than the razor handle, and just deep enough that the razor doesn't topple out. It's kind of weird, like the stand was designed for something else, but I guess it works.

I'm not sure why they call this package a "travelling razor kit." It comes in a large gift box, and doesn't include any sort of travel case or bag. Plus, it's the biggest, heaviest razor I've ever seen (aside from folding straight razors). I wouldn't even think of taking this thing on a trip.

Despite all the good things about this razor, there's one major red flag: At the moment, replacement blades aren't listed on Amazon or on the Suprent website. The package includes two cartridges and a card to get a third for free, but there's no guarantee that more will be available when you need 'em. While the included cartridges are definitely good for several shaves apiece, if that's all you get, you'll soon be left with a very expensive paperweight. I rated the product based on my use of it, but recommend that you verify the availability of replacement cartridges before buying. [3.0/5] Love the ergonomics. Tough to get a close shave though. This is a tough one to review...Works great on my neck and face but not so great on my bald head. The ergonomics are fantastic, it's a hefty metal body with nice curves. The blade nestles in the top groove with metal surrounding it...This makes for a difficult close shave from my bald head curves, I like to press down harder and get a close shave but the surrounding metal prevents that from happening. However, on my neck and cheeks, it does well, particularly with a dry shave.

The foam is nice but nothing special. Doesn't slick up great but has a nice scent. The holder is okay...Sturdy but holds the blade loosely. Also, as of now, I don't see where I can get replacement blades. [4.0/5] Nice for close shave My father shaves his face every other day. I asked him to try this razor and he was really impressed by it. He was able to have a very close shave. I just wish thr blade was more economical to replace. [1.0/5] Looks lux, but that’s about all it has going for it I always wondered why no companies ever made 6 bladed razors, but after using this SUPRENT I think I have an idea…

This razor looks amazingly sleek and stylish. From the blades and the curve of the handle, to the stylish holder. It oozes style and luxury. That is until you actually want to use it…

Just as they claim the handle is alloy and has a good bit of weight to it. But what they don’t tell you is that with wet, sweaty, or shaving foam covered hands this feels like a bar of soap in the shower or one of those slippery snake toys we used to have when I was growing up. The razor head is fixed in what they call an “optimum angle”, except it’s really not. Imagine trying to keep your grip on one of those slippery snakes, while trying to maneuver your hand at all kinds of odd angles to get that “optimum angle”, while 6 nice and sharp blades are around your jugular….

Unfortunately this looks great, but functionally it doesn’t perform. And to be honest that’s a shame since I have used a few different clipper sets from SUPRENT that were amazing all around. This just unfortunately isn’t one of them. [3.0/5] Nice Razor, But Pricy This razor has a very substantial quality feel to it. The angle of the head is a little different than others I have but I find maneuvering with it easy enough on most parts of my face. It gives me a nice close shave on my face and chin. Although it's a bit heavy, I don't find that an impediment. The included base holds the razor upright and is nice looking, with the same substantial feel as the razor itself. The included shave cream is ok.

On the downside, the head is quite large. That made it hard to maneuver in the area between my chin and lower lip. I also couldn't find any information on how to get replacement blades, a must for a manual razor. I looked on the Suprent web site and found the razor but not any blades. Hopefully, that will be corrected soon. Since the design of the blade cartridge is unique, I imagine the cost of the blades will be quite high, as is that of the razor. [5.0/5] Great feeling razor very stoked I try all types of different razors and the ones I grew up using from the major companies are just not made with the same quality as they used to in my opinion. So when I found an interesting shaped kind of new school style I wanted to try it and I'm glad I did. Definitely a comfortable grip and pretty decent quality that's what I was hoping for and I am experiencing satisfaction with these razors. [5.0/5] Nice kit, I like the stand. This razor kit works well for me. I like to bring it when I travel because it comes with a neat stand and the shaving cream. The razor works well and gives me a nice close shave. [3.0/5] Pretty good razor, so-so shaving cream. Not worth the price I usually keep a beard, but I get tired of it every so often and shave the whole thing off. I've been using a straight razor for some time, but these cartridge type razors are definitely easier to use and less likely to produce accidental cuts. I was excited to try this, and in general, it's been pretty good, but not amazing. The razor itself is quite heavy and looks very cool. The cartridge fits easily and is easy to change if necessary. There's also the heavy base piece to stand the razor up. The shaving cream is not that great. It comes out of the can with impressive body, but once you start applying it to your face, it deflates and just doesn't provide awesome coverage. The razor is smooth on the skin, but because of its asymmetrical design, it's less comfortable in the left hand. I'm right handed, but I still switch between hands when I'm shaving different sides of my face. It would have been better if they made it symmetrical. Overall, not really worth $50 despite the cool looks. [5.0/5] Close shave Not a dude but I have plenty of curves so I can testify to how well these work on curves and because of the shape of these they are easy to use on all curves. They have 6 blades and glide effortlessly through even thick hair. Did a great job on shaving large areas and has a nice weight to it so its easy to hold.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

The SUPRENT razor kit receives mixed feedback overall. Many customers praise its premium, luxurious appearance and packaging, with several noting it makes an excellent gift. The six-blade cartridge is frequently highlighted as providing a close, smooth shave, and the heavy metal handle is appreciated by some for its weight and feel in the hand.

However, a recurring and significant complaint is that the handle becomes extremely slippery when wet or covered in shaving cream, making it difficult and potentially dangerous to use. The large razor head is also frequently criticized for making it hard to shave tight areas like around the nose and under the lip. Several reviewers note the fixed head angle can be awkward to work with.

The included shaving cream receives mostly negative feedback, with many finding it thin, ineffective, and not worth using. The stand is generally well-regarded as sturdy and attractive.

The most serious concern raised by multiple reviewers is the apparent lack of available replacement blade cartridges, either on Amazon or the manufacturer's website. This leads some to worry the razor will become useless once the included blades are used up, making the roughly 50 dollar price point hard to justify.

In short, the razor looks and feels premium but has practical usability issues, and the uncertain availability of replacement blades is a major drawback potential buyers should consider before purchasing.
